title: arceuthobium tsugense subsp. contortae
intro: Arceuthobium tsugense subsp. contortae, also known as the contorted dwarf mistletoe, is a type of parasitic plant that grows on the branches of western hemlock trees in North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Arceuthobium tsugense subsp. contortae include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and provide antioxidant protection. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by drying and crushing the plant material, and can be taken as a tea or capsule to support overall health and well-being.
